channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Maine Gov. Janet Mills suspends her U.S. Senate campaign
item.description: The move likely sets up a battle this fall between incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins and progressive Democrat Graham Platner, who is closely aligned with Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders.
item.content:encoded: The move likely sets up a battle this fall between incumbent Republican Sen. Susan Collins and progressive Democrat Graham Platner, who is closely aligned with Vermont Sen. Bernie Sanders.
item.dc:creator: Steve Mistler

item.title: U.S. and Iranian dueling naval blockades are at an almost total standstill
item.description: There's no end in sight for the dueling U.S. and Iranian naval blockades. This raises a host of challenges as for the possibility of an extended standoff or a resumption of hostilities.
item.content:encoded: There's no end in sight for the dueling U.S. and Iranian naval blockades. This raises a host of challenges as for the possibility of an extended standoff or a resumption of hostilities.
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Sen. Angus King on what he plans to ask Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin asks Independent Sen. Angus King of Maine about the questions he plans to ask Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during Hegseth's expected testimony Thursday on Capitol Hill.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Michel Martin asks Independent Sen. Angus King of Maine about the questions he plans to ask Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth during Hegseth's expected testimony Thursday on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Gas prices hit highest point since Iran war began
item.description: Gas prices are at their highest since the war with Iran began.
item.content:encoded: Gas prices are at their highest since the war with Iran began.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: Supreme Court paves the way for largest-ever drop in Black representation in Congress
item.description: By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.
item.content:encoded:
By weakening Voting Rights Act protections against racial discrimination in redistricting, the Supreme Court has paved the way for the largest-ever drop in representation by Black members of Congress.

(Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: After clashing with lawmakers over the Iran war, Pete Hegseth returns for second day of testimony, a look at Fed chair Jerome Powell's legacy, SCOTUS strikes severe blow to Voting Rights Act.
item.content:encoded: After clashing with lawmakers over the Iran war, Pete Hegseth returns for second day of testimony, a look at Fed chair Jerome Powell's legacy, SCOTUS strikes severe blow to Voting Rights Act.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: 'Widow's Bay' creator talks about the new creepy comedy
item.description: In the Apple TV series "Widow's Bay," the mayor of a cursed New England island is trying to boost tourism. NPR's A Martinez speaks with show creator Katie Dippold about the horror-comedy.
item.content:encoded: In the Apple TV series "Widow's Bay," the mayor of a cursed New England island is trying to boost tourism. NPR's A Martinez speaks with show creator Katie Dippold about the horror-comedy.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Long naval deployments take toll on military families
item.description: The impacts of long deployments in the Middle East and the Caribbean are causing an increasing strain on military families in Navy towns like Norfolk, Virginia.
item.content:encoded: The impacts of long deployments in the Middle East and the Caribbean are causing an increasing strain on military families in Navy towns like Norfolk, Virginia.
item.dc:creator: Steve Walsh

item.title: Iran war puts strain on country's already battered economy
item.description: The Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz have damaged the global economy, but Iran's own economy has been faltering for years.
item.content:encoded: The Iran war and closure of the Strait of Hormuz have damaged the global economy, but Iran's own economy has been faltering for years.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Northam

item.title: Chicago to host International Jazz Day
item.description: Chicago is hosting this year's International Jazz Day. NPR's A Martinez speaks with Nate Chinen, who is covering the event for Philadelphia's WRTI jazz station.
item.content:encoded: Chicago is hosting this year's International Jazz Day. NPR's A Martinez speaks with Nate Chinen, who is covering the event for Philadelphia's WRTI jazz station.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez
